contests.race_blurb: Only one candidate filed for this South King County seat around Sea-Tac Airport: appointed incumbent Tina L. Orwall (D), who advances from the primary unopposed. She was appointed in December 2024 to succeed longtime Senator Karen Keiser after 15+ years in the State House.

candidates.summary: Appointed incumbent State Senator and career social worker, serving the 33rd District in the Legislature since 2009, first in the House (including as Speaker Pro Tempore) and in the Senate since December 2024.
highlights: Appointed to the Senate in December 2024 after roughly 15 years as a 33rd District state representative, including service as Speaker Pro Tempore [S1][S3]

Career social worker with twenty years in behavioral health; Vice Chair of the Senate Health & Long-Term Care Committee, with priorities including the 988 crisis line and behavioral-health capacity [S1]
Cites bills prohibiting insurers from using AI to deny care and a 2024 law against non-consensual deepfake intimate images, plus airport pollution mitigation for Sea-Tac communities [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Platform centers on investing in behavioral-health capacity, flood recovery, and backstopping programs threatened by federal cuts.

safety.basis: Behavioral-health career and priorities (988 crisis line, behavioral-health capacity, protecting vulnerable populations) emphasize prevention and services.

experience.basis: In the Legislature since 2009 with House leadership (Speaker Pro Tempore) and Senate committee vice-chairmanship — a long enacted record.

candidates.summary: The appointed incumbent and former Fife councilmember emphasizes public investment in housing, behavioral health, infrastructure, food security, safety, and regional transit.
highlights: Supports affordable housing, behavioral-health and crisis-response funding, transportation safety, and food security [S1][S3]
Opposes delaying or reducing South Sound Sound Transit projects [S2]
Helped adopt the county budget and cites first-year housing, health, infrastructure, and community funding [S3]

spending.basis: His platform and first-year record center on expanded public investment across housing, health, infrastructure, and community services.

housing.basis: Supports county funding for affordable housing, indicating a public-investment approach without a detailed tenant-policy platform.

experience.basis: Combines current county incumbency with eleven years on the Fife City Council and a documented budget record.

candidates.summary: A Puyallup Tribal member and family-business operator emphasizing small-business conditions, public safety, infrastructure, accountability, and scrutiny of taxes and spending.
highlights: Emphasizes small-business conditions, accountability, public safety, and infrastructure [S1][S2]
Calls for scrutiny of taxes and county spending [S1][S2]
Brings tribal, commercial-diving, real-estate, and family-business experience [S1]

taxes.basis: Calls for scrutiny of taxes and county spending, indicating tax restraint without a specific cut proposal.

spending.basis: Emphasizes fiscal restraint and accountability in county spending.

experience.basis: Runs as a private-sector and tribal-community outsider with no documented elected-office record.

contests.office_does: The Pierce County Council adopts county ordinances, budgets, and land-use policy and oversees county services.
contests.race_blurb: The open-seat field includes a school-board member, former Gig Harbor councilmember, police officer and former police-guild president, and a contingency candidate who says she is not actively running. Public safety and fiscal posture provide the clearest contrasts.

candidates.summary: A Peninsula School Board member emphasizing peninsula representation, local services, infrastructure, and responsible budgeting.
highlights: Emphasizes local services and infrastructure [S1]
Calls for responsible budgeting and representation for peninsula communities [S1]

spending.basis: His limited platform explicitly foregrounds responsible budgeting alongside service and infrastructure needs.

experience.basis: Has elected school-board experience but would be new to county legislative office.

candidates.summary: A nurse practitioner, former Gig Harbor councilmember, and Charter Review Commissioner emphasizing public safety, managed growth, infrastructure, affordability, and health-service access.
highlights: Links healthcare experience to behavioral health, emergency response, and service access [S1][S2]
Served on the Gig Harbor City Council and Charter Review Commission [S2][S3]
Endorsed by Washington Conservation Action [S4]

safety.basis: Links public safety to behavioral health, emergency response, and access to services, leaning toward prevention while retaining a broad safety emphasis.

experience.basis: Brings recent municipal governing and regional and charter-review experience to the open county seat.

candidates.summary: A 27-year Seattle police officer and former police-guild president prioritizing deputy staffing, conventional law enforcement, fiscal restraint, bridge replacement, and shoreline and land-use policy.
highlights: Prioritizes deputy staffing and conventional public-safety enforcement [S1][S2]
Calls for limits on rising county costs and overlapping taxes [S1][S2]
Proposes planning to replace the Fox Island bridge and protecting shorelines and docks [S1]

taxes.basis: Explicitly campaigns against rising county costs and overlapping taxes.

spending.basis: Emphasizes fiscal accountability and limiting county costs, while supporting specified infrastructure spending.

safety.basis: Centers deputy staffing and conventional law-enforcement capacity.

experience.basis: Has a long police and labor-leadership record but no documented elected governing tenure.

candidates.summary: A contingency candidate who says she is not actively running and supports Mike Solan.
highlights: Filed while Solan's ballot eligibility was disputed [S2]
Says she is not actively running and hopes to receive no votes [S1][S2]

contests.office_does: Pierce County District Court hears misdemeanors, gross misdemeanors, traffic infractions, protection orders, and limited-jurisdiction civil matters.
contests.race_blurb: Three candidates seek an open judicial position: Eric J. Lawless, longtime deputy prosecutor and judge pro tem Mike Sommerfeld, and former municipal judge and Superior Court commissioner Pam Nogueira.

candidates.summary: A legal professional pledging impartial application of law, respectful courtroom treatment, and attention to both public safety and individual rights.
highlights: Pledges impartial application of law [S1]
Emphasizes respectful treatment, public safety, and individual rights [S1]

candidates.summary: A deputy prosecutor since 1995 and judge pro tem supporting language access, therapeutic courts, barrier reduction, bias-aware practice, and efficient resolution consistent with defense rights.
highlights: Supports language access, reducing barriers, therapeutic courts, and representative juries [S3]
Says efficiency must remain consistent with constitutional rights and defense preparation [S3]
Has three decades of criminal and civil county-law experience plus judge-pro-tem service [S2][S3]

judicial.basis: Supports access reforms, bias-aware practice, representative juries, and therapeutic courts while emphasizing constitutional process.

safety.basis: His explicit support for therapeutic courts places some weight on treatment-oriented responses despite a prosecutorial background.

experience.basis: Has three decades of criminal and civil county-law practice and judge-pro-tem service, though not full-time judicial tenure.

candidates.summary: A former Olympia Municipal Court judge and Superior Court commissioner whose record emphasizes accessible courts, therapeutic courts, judicial education, and court administration.
highlights: Only candidate with documented full-time judicial and commissioner service [S2][S4]
Served on committees involving therapeutic courts, protection orders, education, jury instructions, diversity, and jail modernization [S2][S3]
Emphasizes fair treatment and accessible courts [S1][S2]

judicial.basis: Her documented emphasis on accessible and therapeutic courts reflects a pragmatic, reform-open judicial posture.

safety.basis: Therapeutic-court work supports a treatment-oriented public-safety approach without evidence for a maximal placement.

experience.basis: Only candidate with documented full-time judge and court-commissioner service, plus court-administration and education work.

contests.office_does: The auditor administers elections and vote-by-mail, maintains public records, manages licensing, and oversees county financial processes.
contests.race_blurb: Jeremy Harrison-Smith emphasizes election access and administrative modernization; former councilmember Mark Hulst emphasizes management experience, legal compliance, and nonpartisan administration.

candidates.summary: Harrison-Smith is a regional government representative and former nonprofit leader running on election access and administrative modernization.
highlights: Supports vote-by-mail, expanded language access, and voter outreach [S1]
Calls for modern financial systems and improved licensing and records access [S1]

experience.basis: He brings relevant government and nonprofit administration but is a new candidate for the auditor's office.

candidates.summary: Hulst is a former Mount Vernon councilmember who emphasizes management experience, legal compliance, and politically independent administration.
highlights: Reports 12 years of city-council service, including finance and public-safety chair roles [S1]
Emphasizes accurate elections, transparent processes, trained staff, and strict legal compliance [S1]

experience.basis: Twelve years in elected city government and related board and management work give him an established public-sector record.

contests.office_does: County commissioners adopt the county budget and policy, oversee departments and infrastructure, and administer land-use, health, safety, and human-service functions.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Joe Burns emphasizes affordability and current county projects; Connie Krier runs on independent oversight; state senator and former mayor Keith Wagoner emphasizes regulatory and tax relief.

candidates.summary: Krier is a safety professional and former small-business owner running on transparency, efficiency, and independent oversight.
highlights: Centers transparency, public input, operational safety, efficiency, and accountability [S1]
Says she would serve independently of party leadership [S1]

spending.basis: Her stated governing agenda leads with efficiency and accountability rather than program expansion.

experience.basis: She brings professional and small-business leadership but presents as a new independent voice in county government.

candidates.summary: Burns is the incumbent commissioner and a former Sedro-Woolley councilmember who emphasizes affordability, growth planning, and local agriculture and business.
highlights: Prioritizes affordability, taxpayer protection, responsible growth, local business, and agriculture [S1]
Says he secured grants and strengthened flood protection as commissioner [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly lists taxpayer protection among his governing priorities.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent commissioner with prior city-council service and cites completed county work.

candidates.summary: Wagoner is a state senator and former Sedro-Woolley mayor and councilmember running on tax and regulatory relief, infrastructure, and rural representation.
highlights: Supports reducing tax and regulatory burdens on farms and businesses [S1]
Emphasizes rural representation, infrastructure funding, and pragmatic administration [S1]
Has served in the state Senate since 2018 after terms as mayor and councilmember [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports reducing tax burdens on farms and businesses.

experience.basis: His record spans state Senate service and prior terms as mayor and city councilmember.

contests.office_does: A PUD commissioner sets policy for the customer-owned utility, including rates, reliability, resource planning, and utility services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Sid Logan faces Bruce King and former Island County commissioner Janet St Clair in a race centered on rates, reliability, resource planning, and clean-energy investment.

candidates.summary: Logan is the incumbent PUD commissioner, emphasizing rates, reliability, cybersecurity, renewable integration, and customer assistance.
highlights: Has served as PUD commissioner since 2017 and is an engineer and Energy Northwest director [S1]
Supports low rates, reliability, cyber preparation, renewable integration, and customer assistance [S1][S2]

candidates.summary: King is a technology entrepreneur and farmer emphasizing low-cost reliable service, broad resource options, and greater public input.
highlights: Reports decades of small-business experience [S1]
Supports planning across nuclear, solar, hydro, and fossil options with greater public input [S1][S2]

candidates.summary: St Clair is a former Island County commissioner emphasizing affordability, reliable service, and investment in water and energy resources.
highlights: Served eight years as Island County commissioner and previously led nonprofits [S1]
Promises low bills and reliable service alongside expanded water and energy investment [S1][S2]

contests.office_does: The auditor administers elections and voter registration, records property documents, licenses vehicles and marriages, and performs county financial and payroll functions.
contests.race_blurb: In this open race, Callie Gee and Dale Whitaker foreground accounting credentials, while Michael Cathcart emphasizes elected management, oversight, and open-government work.

candidates.summary: A public accountant, Gee emphasizes election access, vote-by-mail protection, transparent reporting, and private-sector financial management.
highlights: Works as a public accountant and has accounting and finance degrees [S1][S3]
Supports vote-by-mail protection, broad participation, and independent election oversight [S1][S2]
Promises transparent and fiscally responsible administration [S1]

spending.basis: She centers financial reporting, compliance, transparency, and fiscal responsibility.

experience.basis: She has professional accounting experience but no prior county-office management record.

candidates.summary: An enrolled agent and tax-practice owner, Whitaker presents the auditor primarily as an accounting and fraud-detection office.
highlights: Reports 18 years of accounting experience and prior corporate CFO work [S1][S3]
Proposes readable statements, modernized services, and close stewardship of public funds [S1][S2]
Emphasizes election security rather than an access-oriented program [S1]

spending.basis: His platform centers accounting controls, fraud detection, and close stewardship of existing funds.

experience.basis: He has extensive accounting and CFO experience but no county auditor management record.

candidates.summary: A Spokane city councilmember, Cathcart emphasizes his elected budget oversight, regional-board service, fraud prevention, and open-government record.
highlights: Has served on the Spokane City Council since 2020 with finance and regional-board roles [S1][S3]
Promises financial scrutiny, fraud prevention, election integrity, and public access to facts [S1][S2]
Points to budget oversight and open-government work as his relevant record [S1]

spending.basis: He repeatedly frames the office around scrutiny, fraud prevention, oversight, and public accountability.

experience.basis: He has the field's strongest elected and intergovernmental management record, though not direct auditor service.

contests.office_does: The clerk maintains Superior Court records, receives filings and fees, supports courtroom administration, and manages lawful public access to records.
contests.race_blurb: Dave Lucas emphasizes court-administration experience and continuity; Elliot Robison emphasizes technology, accessibility, workplace relations, and operational reporting.

candidates.summary: Cheney's municipal court administrator, Lucas presents direct court-management and military leadership experience as preparation for the clerk's office.
highlights: Serves as Cheney Municipal Court administrator and completed the state Court Administrators Academy [S1]
Proposes continued modernization with attention to speed, accuracy, and confidential information [S1]

experience.basis: He has direct court-administration experience, though not prior elected county-clerk service.

candidates.summary: A business-intelligence analyst, Robison proposes technology-driven workflow improvements and expanded public access tools.
highlights: Proposes expanded guides, notifications, translated forms, and public operational reporting [S1][S2]
Would apply capacity analysis and supports a union-friendly workplace [S1]

experience.basis: He offers relevant analytics and software experience but no direct court-administration record.

contests.office_does: The prosecutor leads felony and juvenile prosecution, represents the county in civil matters, advises county government, and sets charging and diversion priorities within state law.
contests.race_blurb: Both candidates are deputy prosecutors. Danny Tarkenton emphasizes psychology-informed recidivism reduction and political independence, while incumbent Preston McCollam emphasizes enforcement, victim advocacy, and institutional continuity.

candidates.summary: A deputy prosecutor and Iraq War veteran, Tarkenton emphasizes behavioral evidence, recidivism reduction, and independence from party organizations.
highlights: Handles criminal and involuntary-treatment proceedings and reports a docket exceeding 500 cases [S1]
Says psychology-informed prosecution can reduce repeat offending [S1][S2]
Frames independence from political parties as important to prosecutorial discretion [S1]

safety.basis: Her public-safety approach emphasizes reducing recidivism through psychology-informed prosecution.

experience.basis: She has substantial current prosecutorial responsibility but not the incumbent's senior management record.

candidates.summary: The incumbent prosecutor, McCollam emphasizes conventional enforcement, crime-victim advocacy, and continuity in senior office management.
highlights: Has served as county prosecutor since July 2025 after more than a decade in criminal and civil prosecution [S1]
Promises law-enforcement collaboration, offender accountability, and victim advocacy [S1][S2]
Previously served as chief criminal deputy and interim civil chief [S1]

safety.basis: He repeatedly centers law-enforcement collaboration, offender accountability, and conventional prosecution.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent with more than a decade of prosecution and senior office-management experience.

contests.office_does: The assessor values taxable property, maintains assessment records, and administers property-tax exemptions.
contests.race_blurb: JJ Olson offers direct appraisal and assessment-technology experience, while Lynda Nashed Zeman emphasizes assessor-office and county executive administration.

candidates.summary: Olson is an assessor-office appraisal and tax-software manager emphasizing transparent valuations, accessible systems, and a technology transition.
highlights: Has worked as an appraiser, analyst, and assessment-software manager [S1]
Promises fair, transparent appraisals and accessible systems [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has direct appraisal, analysis, and assessor-system management experience.

candidates.summary: Zeman offers assessor-office and broader county executive experience, with priorities in succession, training, modernization, and valuation communication.
highlights: Has county executive-administration and assessor-office experience [S1]
Prioritizes succession, training, modernization, and clearer valuation communication [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has direct assessor-office and wider county administrative experience.

contests.office_does: The county clerk maintains superior-court records, processes filings and court orders, and supports public access to the court system.
contests.race_blurb: Deputy clerk Garrett Cady emphasizes direct office systems experience, while Nicole Miller emphasizes broader public-service management, accessibility, and workplace culture.

candidates.summary: Miller brings nearly 30 years in public service and proposes expanded in-person access, backlog reduction, and workplace improvements.
highlights: Reports nearly 30 years in public service, child welfare, juvenile justice, and equity administration [S1]
Would expand access, address backlogs, and improve workplace culture [S1][S2]

experience.basis: She has a long public-service management record, though not direct clerk-office service.

candidates.summary: Cady is a deputy clerk with court-systems experience who emphasizes access, staff retention, restored assistance, and continuity.
highlights: Works as a deputy clerk and has experience in the office's principal information systems [S1]
Prioritizes access, retention, restored domestic-violence assistance, and in-person service [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He has direct deputy-clerk and court-systems experience.

contests.office_does: County commissioners adopt the county budget and laws and oversee land use, public health, public safety, infrastructure, and county services.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Emily Clouse emphasizes housing and climate investments, Lacey Councilmember Nicolas Martinez-Dunning emphasizes fiscal restraint and reform, and Michelle Gipson emphasizes housing, safety, families, and managed growth.

candidates.summary: Martinez-Dunning is a Lacey councilmember campaigning on deficits, transparency, cross-party management, and independence from PAC funding.
highlights: Serves on the Lacey City Council and has business, nonprofit, and military experience [S1]
Highlights deficits and trust concerns and rejects PAC and special-interest funding [S1][S2]

spending.basis: He centers county deficits, fiscal management, and accountability.

experience.basis: He has current elected municipal experience plus business and nonprofit management.

reform.basis: He rejects PAC and special-interest funding and emphasizes transparency and trust reform.

candidates.summary: Clouse is the incumbent commissioner, citing housing, supportive-service, climate, and restoration investments alongside process-accountability concerns.
highlights: Cites $25 million for affordable housing and 186 supportive units [S1][S2]
Cites heat-pump discounts and estuary restoration [S1][S2]
Opposed a budget process she said lacked public input [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Her record centers substantial public investment in housing, supportive units, and climate programs.

housing.basis: She cites direct county funding for affordable housing and supportive units.

climate.basis: She cites heat-pump discounts and estuary restoration as core record items.

experience.basis: She is the incumbent with an established county governing record.

candidates.summary: Gipson is a school-board director and nonprofit operations executive emphasizing housing, community wellbeing, families, environmental protection, and responsible growth.
highlights: Serves on the North Thurston School Board and works in nonprofit operations [S1]
Supports affordable housing, community safety, youth and families, environmental protection, and responsible growth [S1][S2]

housing.basis: She explicitly supports affordable housing, though the dossier does not specify the policy mechanism.

experience.basis: She has elected school-governance and nonprofit operations experience.

contests.office_does: The sheriff leads county law enforcement, patrol and investigations, civil process, and related public-safety operations.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Derek Sanders emphasizes de-escalation and partnerships; detective Kevin Burton-Crow emphasizes accreditation, published rules, victim-centered training, and budget disclosure.

candidates.summary: Burton-Crow is a special-victims detective and union leader proposing accreditation, policy transparency, victim-centered training, and budget disclosure.
highlights: Has served as a special-victims, patrol, and reserve deputy and as a military leader [S1]
Proposes accreditation, published policies, victim-centered and harm-reduction training, and annual budget disclosure [S1][S2]

safety.basis: His safety platform specifically includes victim-centered and harm-reduction training alongside professional standards.

experience.basis: He has extensive deputy and military experience but is the challenger to agency leadership.

candidates.summary: Sanders is the incumbent sheriff, emphasizing de-escalation, public communication, community relationships, and regional partnerships.
highlights: Has served as sheriff since 2022 after work as a community-service officer and deputy [S1]
Emphasizes de-escalation, communication, community relationships, and regional partnerships [S1][S2]

safety.basis: He explicitly centers de-escalation, community relationships, and partnerships in public safety.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent sheriff with prior front-line agency experience.

contests.office_does: The prosecutor brings criminal cases, represents and advises the county, and manages charging, diversion, victim-service, and office policy.
contests.race_blurb: Four attorneys compete for the open office, differing in enforcement, diversion, management, and the balance between prosecution and defense experience.

candidates.summary: Corkern is a senior civil deputy with defense and litigation experience emphasizing consistent charging, management reform, partnerships, fairness, and transparency.
highlights: Has civil-deputy, criminal-defense, and litigation experience [S1][S2][S3]
Proposes consistent charging, training, supervision, reduced delays, and balanced caseloads [S2][S3]

safety.basis: He emphasizes consistent charging and law-enforcement partnerships alongside fairness and management reform.

experience.basis: He has direct senior prosecutor-office, defense, and civil-litigation experience.

candidates.summary: Sigmar is the chief criminal deputy, combining prosecution leadership with diversion, rehabilitation, root-cause work, and victim justice.
highlights: Has about 15 years in prosecution and more than seven leading the felony division [S1][S2][S3]
Combines accountability with rehabilitation, diversion, and root-cause work [S2][S3]

safety.basis: He explicitly pairs accountability with rehabilitation, diversion, and root-cause approaches.

experience.basis: He has long prosecution tenure and current felony-division leadership.

candidates.summary: Swinburnson is a defense attorney and school-board president with prior prosecution experience, emphasizing balanced decisions, trust, safety, and leadership.
highlights: Has more than 26 years across prosecution and defense [S1][S2][S3]
Serves as Blaine School Board president and emphasizes balanced decision-making [S2][S3]

safety.basis: His platform explicitly presents a balanced prosecution-and-defense approach to public safety.

experience.basis: He combines a long legal record with elected school-board leadership, though not prosecutor-office executive service.

candidates.summary: Bracke is an assistant chief criminal deputy emphasizing serious-case accountability, victims, safety, and close law-enforcement partnership.
highlights: Oversees District Court as an assistant chief criminal deputy [S1][S2][S3]
Emphasizes serious-case accountability, victim support, and law-enforcement partnership [S2][S3]

safety.basis: Her platform centers serious-case prosecution, accountability, and law-enforcement partnership.

experience.basis: She has direct criminal-deputy leadership and prosecution experience.

contests.office_does: The assessor inventories and values taxable property and administers exemptions.
contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Jacob Tate faces challenger Keith Effler; substantive policy evidence is sparse.

candidates.summary: The current assessor; available evidence establishes incumbency but no detailed 2026 platform.
highlights: County office page confirms his incumbency [S2]

experience.basis: Current officeholder with direct assessor experience.

candidates.summary: A filed challenger with no verified substantive platform or assessment-administration record.

contests.race_blurb: Incumbent Ross faces Cuevas and Barker.

candidates.summary: Educator proposing bilingual access, reporting, operational review, and digital services.
highlights: Educator proposing bilingual access, reporting, operational review, and digital services. [S1]

spending.basis: Operational review and expenditure transparency.

experience.basis: No auditor-office management.

candidates.summary: Incumbent auditor with no detailed platform.
highlights: Incumbent auditor with no detailed platform. [S1]

highlights: Challenger with insufficient stable platform evidence. [S1]

contests.race_blurb: Four names remain; Mullen suspended after the withdrawal deadline.

candidates.summary: Candidate with insufficient platform evidence.
highlights: Candidate with insufficient platform evidence. [S1]

candidates.summary: Sheriff emphasizing safety, agriculture, water, budgets, and cooperation.
highlights: Sheriff emphasizing safety, agriculture, water, budgets, and cooperation. [S1]

spending.basis: Budget accountability.

safety.basis: Sheriff and safety priority.

experience.basis: Elected sheriff and long record.

candidates.summary: Ballot-listed candidate who suspended campaigning after the withdrawal deadline.
highlights: Ballot-listed candidate who suspended campaigning after the withdrawal deadline. [S1]

candidates.summary: Candidate with insufficient platform evidence.
highlights: Candidate with insufficient platform evidence. [S1]

contests.office_does: The prosecutor brings criminal cases, advises county government, and represents the county in civil matters.
contests.race_blurb: Two attorneys differ most clearly on enforcement specificity and breadth of legal background.

candidates.summary: Attorney with prosecution, defense, civil, and pro-tem judging experience emphasizing fairness, accountability, and trust.
highlights: Has worked on prosecution and defense sides [S2]
Platform is principle-oriented with limited operational detail [S2]

experience.basis: Broad legal and pro-tem judicial experience, though no elected prosecutor tenure.

candidates.summary: Former special-assault prosecutor and current assistant city attorney emphasizing gangs, repeat violent offenders, victims, and law-enforcement coordination.
highlights: Prioritizes gang enforcement and repeat violent-offender prosecution [S2]
Calls for stronger victim services and law-enforcement coordination [S2]

safety.basis: Explicit enforcement-first agenda centered on gangs and repeat violent offenders.

experience.basis: Direct prosecutorial and municipal-law experience.

contests.office_does: The sheriff provides patrol, investigation, civil process, and emergency response across the county.
contests.race_blurb: Both candidates emphasize staffing, drugs, transparency, and agency leadership; Church has the more fully corroborated record.

candidates.summary: Sheriff's lieutenant with 28 years in law enforcement emphasizing training, equipment, staffing, professional standards, enforcement, and prevention.
highlights: Oversees standards, training, and policy [S2][S3]
Combines enforcement against drugs and property crime with evidence-based prevention [S2]

safety.basis: Explicitly combines enforcement with evidence-based prevention.

experience.basis: Long local law-enforcement and current command record.

candidates.summary: Law-enforcement candidate emphasizing patrol coverage, recruiting, training, leadership, transparency, and fentanyl enforcement.
highlights: Calls for greater patrol coverage and recruitment [S2]
Emphasizes enforcement against fentanyl and illegal drugs [S2]

safety.basis: Concrete enforcement-first priorities include patrol expansion and drug enforcement.

contests.office_does: The treasurer collects and distributes taxes and manages county cash, investments, and banking.
contests.race_blurb: Office investment and banking manager Stephanie Patterson faces Sam Johnson, for whom no substantive qualifications were verified.

candidates.summary: Senior county investments and banking manager emphasizing internal controls, data security, software modernization, reporting, and cross-training.
highlights: Has held the office's senior investment and banking role since 2014 [S3]
Supports controls, cross-training, and modernized systems [S2][S3]

spending.basis: Internal controls, centralized receipting, and resilience emphasize accountability.

experience.basis: Long office-specific management of core treasury functions.
